overview.tagline: A welcoming suburban borough with a vibrant Main Street and outdoor spirit
overview.description: Ramsey is a charming borough in northern Bergen County that blends small-town character with modern suburban living. Known for its walkable Main Street, strong community events, and proximity to the Ramapo Valley and Campgaw Mountain, Ramsey attracts families seeking excellent schools, safe neighborhoods, and easy access to both outdoor recreation and New York City. The borough also serves as the home of the Darlington County Park and several corporate headquarters.
highlights: Walkable Main Street with local shops, restaurants, and cafes
Home to Campgaw Mountain Reservation for skiing and hiking
NJ Transit Pascack Valley Line and Main Line stations
Strong community with numerous annual events and festivals
Corporate hub with Konica Minolta and other major employers

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. Ramsey features a diverse housing stock including colonials, split-levels, ranches, and newer construction.
realEstate.recentTrends: Ramsey's real estate market has been strong with steady price appreciation, driven by its school district, community amenities, and relative affordability compared to southern Bergen towns. Homes near the downtown and train station command premium prices. The market has seen increased demand from families relocating from more urban areas.
neighborhoodNotes: Main Street area — Walking distance to shops, restaurants, and the train station
North Ramsey — Larger lots and newer construction near Campgaw Mountain
South Ramsey — Established neighborhoods with split-levels and ranches
Darlington area — Properties near the county park and lake
West Ramsey — Quiet residential streets near the Mahwah border

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by the Borough of Ramsey.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days by zone
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids, maximum 50 lbs per container
Bulk item pickup available by appointment with the DPW
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Single-stream curbside recycling is collected weekly.
details: Commingled recyclables (glass, cans, plastic #1-#7) collected weekly
Paper and cardboard collected on recycling day
Recycling bins provided by the Borough
Yard waste collected seasonally from April through December
Electronics recycling drop-off available at DPW yard

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water service is provided by Veolia Water and sewer service is maintained by the Borough.
details: Water supplied by Veolia Water (formerly United Water/Suez)
Sewer service maintained by the Borough DPW
Water billing handled by Veolia quarterly
Annual water quality reports available online
Emergency water line repairs available 24/7 through Veolia

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning approvals, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for new construction, additions, and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board and Zoning Board
Permit applications available online and in person at Borough Hall
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Borough operates commuter parking and downtown municipal lots.
details: Commuter parking permits available for train station lots
Free parking available in municipal lots downtown
Metered parking along Main Street with time limits
Overnight parking restrictions on borough streets
Snow emergency parking regulations enforced

additionalServices.title: Ramsey Free Public Library
additionalServices.description: Full-service public library offering books, digital resources, programs, and community meeting spaces.
details: Located at 30 Wyckoff Avenue
Children's programs, teen activities, and adult book clubs
Free Wi-Fi, computer access, and meeting rooms
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
additionalServices.description: Year-round recreational programs for residents of all ages.
details: Youth sports leagues including soccer, baseball, and basketball
Summer day camp programs at Finch Park
Adult fitness programs and senior activities
Community pool membership available seasonally

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Ramsey enforces noise regulations to maintain the residential character of the borough.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM only
No construction noise permitted on Sundays or federal holidays
Leaf blowers and power equipment restricted to certain hours
Loud music and gatherings subject to noise limits after 10:00 PM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain properties in good condition.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained and grass kept at a reasonable height
Property owners responsible for sidewalk snow removal within 24 hours
Fences must not exceed 6 feet in rear yards and 4 feet in front yards
Abandoned or inoperable vehicles must be removed from property
Swimming pools must be properly fenced and maintained

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Ramsey requires dog licensing and enforces leash laws throughout the borough.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with proof of rabies vaccination
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately by the owner
Excessive barking may result in violation notices
No exotic or wild animals permitted as pets

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Street parking is regulated throughout the borough.
keyRules: No overnight parking on borough streets between 2:00 AM and 6:00 AM without permit
Snow emergency parking bans enforced — vehicles must be off-street
Commuter parking lots require valid permits
Commercial vehicles over 4 tons prohibited on residential streets
Time-limited parking in downtown zone enforced

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must be registered and comply with housing codes.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Borough
Certificate of Habitability required before occupancy
Landlords must maintain properties to housing code standards
Short-term rentals (under 30 days) are not permitted in residential zones
Inspections conducted at change of tenancy

other.title: Swimming Pool Regulations
other.summary: Private swimming pools must comply with safety and setback requirements.
keyRules: Pools must be enclosed by a fence at least 4 feet high with a self-closing gate
Building permit required for pool installation
Pools must meet setback requirements from property lines
Pool water discharge must not impact neighboring properties

publicSchools.name: Hubbard Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Primary school serving the younger elementary students

publicSchools.name: Eric S. Smith Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Middle school with strong academics and extracurricular programs

publicSchools.name: Ramsey High School
publicSchools.notes: Well-regarded high school with competitive athletics and college preparatory programs

privateSchools.name: St. Paul School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic school in the Archdiocese of Newark

privateSchools.name: Don Bosco Preparatory High School
privateSchools.notes: All-boys Catholic preparatory school known for strong academics and championship athletics programs

nearbyHigherEd: Ramapo College of New Jersey (Mahwah) — 10 minutes

whyMoveHere: Strong public school district with a community-focused atmosphere
Walkable Main Street with local shops, restaurants, and services
NJ Transit train service on two lines (Main Line and Pascack Valley)
Close to Campgaw Mountain for skiing, snowboarding, and hiking
Family-oriented community with numerous annual events and activities

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NJ Transit Main Line / Pascack Valley Line
commuteToNYC.duration: 55-70 minutes to Hoboken or Suffern connection
commuteToNYC.trainStation: Ramsey Station (Main Street) and Ramsey Route 17 Station
commuteToNYC.notes: Ramsey is served by two train stations — Ramsey on Main Street (Main Line) and Ramsey Route 17 (limited service). The Main Line runs to Hoboken Terminal. Bus 165 provides service to the Port Authority Bus Terminal. Route 17 provides highway access to the Lincoln Tunnel during off-peak hours.

costOfLiving.summary: Ramsey offers a moderate cost of living by Bergen County standards, balancing good schools and amenities with relatively accessible home prices.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 30% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Strong school system with dedicated community support
Charming Main Street with local character
Two NJ Transit train stations for commuter flexibility
Access to Campgaw Mountain and outdoor recreation
cons: Longer commute to NYC compared to southern Bergen County
Property taxes are high relative to home values
Route 17 traffic can cause congestion during peak hours
Limited public transportation options beyond the train
Some areas of the borough have older housing stock

restaurants.name: Cafe Amici
restaurants.description: Popular Italian restaurant serving classic pasta dishes, brick-oven pizza, and seasonal specials in a cozy setting.

restaurants.name: The Station Bar & Grill
restaurants.description: Casual bar and grill near the train station offering burgers, pub fare, and a lively atmosphere.

restaurants.name: Sakura Japanese Restaurant
restaurants.description: Japanese restaurant offering fresh sushi, sashimi, and traditional Japanese cuisine.

restaurants.name: Ramsey Tap House
restaurants.description: Craft beer-focused gastropub with a rotating selection of taps and upscale pub food.

restaurants.name: Suburbia Diner
restaurants.description: Classic New Jersey diner with an extensive menu serving breakfast all day, plus lunch and dinner favorites.

parks.name: Campgaw Mountain Reservation
parks.description: Bergen County park featuring ski slopes, hiking trails, disc golf, and mountain biking. The only ski area in Bergen County.

parks.name: Finch Park
parks.description: Borough's primary recreation park with baseball fields, soccer fields, tennis courts, a playground, and a community pool.

parks.name: Ramsey Reservoir & Hiking Trails
parks.description: Scenic reservoir area with walking and hiking trails through wooded areas, popular with joggers and nature enthusiasts.

parks.name: Valley Social Park
parks.description: Neighborhood park with picnic areas, playground equipment, and open green space.

events.name: Ramsey Day
events.description: Annual borough-wide celebration featuring live music, food vendors, carnival rides, craft booths, and fireworks.

events.name: Ramsey Farmers Market
events.description: Weekly outdoor market offering fresh produce, baked goods, artisan foods, and locally made products.

events.name: Memorial Day Parade
events.description: Annual parade through downtown Ramsey honoring veterans, featuring local organizations, scouts, and marching bands.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting & Santa Visit
events.description: Annual holiday celebration on Main Street with tree lighting, caroling, hot cocoa, and a visit from Santa.

attractions.name: Main Street Ramsey
attractions.description: Walkable downtown strip with independently owned shops, restaurants, and services giving the borough its small-town charm.

attractions.name: Campgaw Mountain Reservation
attractions.description: Year-round outdoor recreation area offering skiing and snowboarding in winter, hiking and mountain biking in warmer months.

attractions.name: Old Stone House (Historical)
attractions.description: Historic stone house dating to the colonial era, preserved as a local landmark reflecting Ramsey's early history.

shoppingAreas: Main Street — Local shops, cafes, and services in the downtown core
Interstate Shopping Center (Route 17) — Major retail and shopping
Ramsey Square — Small shopping plaza with convenience stores and services
Garden State Plaza (Paramus) — Major regional mall nearby

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the 2nd and 4th Wednesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Borough Hall, 33 North Central Avenue, Ramsey, NJ 07446

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital (Valley Health System)
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
